profile_bio.description: prop trader

kept.text:
I set up a contextual retrieval system yesterday that bridged slack/gdocs/dashboard/database/datadog/main repos across two VMs and my personal computer and 3 or 4 agents.

Going between all the agents who were sharing context and solving my problems was not oldschool coding flow state, but it had a sort of flow vibe that was beautiful in its own right.

profile_bio.description: post-ironic post-rationalist https://t.co/1QoefmwUlA

quoted_tweet.text:
Programming used to put me into FLOW state often. That’s a big part of why I fell in love with it. Late night missions building games, apps, whatever, it felt great. 

Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi has a great quote on this:

“The best moments in our lives… occur when a person’s body or mind is stretched to its limits in a voluntary effort to accomplish something difficult or worthwhile.”

AI has kind of nuked the personal challenge part of that. I don’t fall into those same FLOW states nearly as often anymore. It’s a strange position to be in because I’m undeniably more productive, but some of the joy is gone. I still love building software, but now the enjoyment comes more from seeing products come to life and have real-world impact than from the actual process of writing the code.

I’ll miss the deep flow states that came from fighting code for hours into the night, but I feel like I do need to find new areas that still offer that same sense of challenge.

kept.text:
Telemetry ingestion is accelerating as enterprises scale AI workloads directly inside observability platforms. That shift is turning $DDOG and $DT into infrastructure layers for agentic systems.

JPMorgan sees AI observability as a major driver of bill expansion over the next 12-24 months. Much of that demand is coming from telemetry feeding LLM workflows rather than legacy monitoring.

At the same time, ingestion cost pressure is pushing some hyperscalers toward alternatives like ClickHouse, where $NBIS holds a 28% stake. The category remains early but structurally important as AI-native monitoring expands.
